rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when they are visible in the scope of the annex.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and should follow valid AGREE syntax for constants, equations, assumptions, and guarantees.
Component type contracts should not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not assume that AADL property names like Period or Deadline are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax notes for annex placement
